name: Jeffrey Morgan
bio: Jeffrey Morgan is the Co-Founder and CEO of Ollama, the leading open-source platform for running large language models locally. Ollama's GitHub repository has amassed over 165,000 stars, making it one of the fastest-growing open-source projects in history and the winner of GitHub's inaugural Awesome AI Award in 2024. Before Ollama, Morgan co-founded Kitematic, a Mac tool for simplifying Docker container management that was acquired by Docker in March 2015. He then served as a Senior Engineer and Product Manager at Docker from 2014 to 2019. He subsequently co-founded Infra (2021-2023), an infrastructure access management startup. Morgan holds a Bachelor of Software Engineering from the University of Waterloo (2008-2013) and had early career stints at Google and Twitter. He co-founded Ollama in 2023 with Michael Chiang; the company graduated from Y Combinator's W21 batch and is headquartered in Palo Alto.

timeline.event: Enrolled in Bachelor of Software Engineering at University of Waterloo
Co-founded Kitematic, a Mac GUI for managing Docker containers, while at University of Waterloo
Kitematic acquired by Docker Inc.; the tool had garnered 2,600+ GitHub stars in six months
Joined Docker Inc. as Senior Engineer and Product Manager following Kitematic acquisition
Left Docker after five years; contributed to Docker Toolbox and container developer experience
Co-founded Infra, an infrastructure access management startup focused on safe, automated provisioning
Launched Ollama publicly with initial support for Llama 2; first blog post 'Run Llama 2 uncensored locally'
Added Code Llama support to Ollama, expanding local coding assistant capabilities
Ollama released as an official Docker image, bridging containerization and local LLM inference
Released official Ollama Python and JavaScript client libraries
Shipped OpenAI API compatibility layer, enabling drop-in replacement for OpenAI-based applications
Launched Windows preview, bringing Ollama to the Windows ecosystem
Added AMD GPU support, expanding beyond NVIDIA-only hardware
Introduced embedding model support for search and RAG applications
Shipped tool/function calling support for Ollama models
Released structured outputs support, enabling JSON schema-constrained generation
Ollama surpassed 105,000 GitHub stars (261% growth in 2024) and won GitHub's inaugural Awesome AI Award
Published 'Minions: where local and cloud LLMs meet' -- hybrid local-cloud inference paradigm
Launched new engine for multimodal models, expanding beyond text to vision capabilities
Introduced cloud models alongside local models, enabling seamless local-cloud switching
Launched 'ollama launch' -- integrated agent runner for Claude Code and OpenClaw
Ollama repository reaches 165,600+ GitHub stars; supports Kimi-K2.5, DeepSeek, Qwen, Gemma and dozens more model families

key_contributions.title: Ollama
key_contributions.description: Open-source platform for running LLMs locally with a simple CLI (ollama run). 165,600+ GitHub stars, winner of GitHub's inaugural Awesome AI Award (2024). Supports dozens of model families including Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen, Gemma, Mistral, and more across macOS, Linux, and Windows.

key_contributions.title: Ollama Python Library
key_contributions.description: Official Python client for the Ollama API, enabling programmatic access to local LLM inference. 9,600+ GitHub stars.

key_contributions.title: Ollama JavaScript Library
key_contributions.description: Official JavaScript/TypeScript client for Ollama, powering web and Node.js integrations with local models. 4,070+ GitHub stars.

key_contributions.title: Kitematic (acquired by Docker)
key_contributions.description: Mac GUI for managing Docker containers that reduced Docker setup from 30-60 minutes to a few minutes. Co-founded with Michael Chiang and Sean Li while at University of Waterloo. Acquired by Docker in March 2015.

key_contributions.title: Infra
key_contributions.description: Infrastructure access management platform for safe, consistent, and fully automated provisioning. Co-founded in 2021.

key_contributions.title: OpenAI API Compatibility Layer
key_contributions.description: Drop-in API compatibility with OpenAI's chat completions format, allowing existing OpenAI-based applications to switch to local models with minimal code changes.

key_contributions.title: Minions: Local-Cloud Hybrid Inference
key_contributions.description: Research-driven approach combining local and cloud LLMs for privacy-preserving yet capable AI workflows, including the 'Secure Minions' extension.

quotes.text: Get up and running with large language models.

quotes.source: X/Twitter announcement of function calling support
X/Twitter post demonstrating one-command local DeepSeek R1 inference
